## Overview

ACT-R is a cognitive architecture that models human cognition through declarative memory and procedural memory modules. Its memory model formalizes retrieval dynamics with base-level activation and [[Spreading Activation]] from the current context.

## Memory dynamics

The architecture combines recency, frequency, and contextual cueing — making it the most mathematically precise theory of how memories become accessible. ACT-R's predictions about memory retrieval times match empirical data across decades of experiments.
